report_name: Spanish Construction Giant Grupo Fonsán Hit by 'Booba' Threat Group

threat_actors.name: Booba
threat_actors.description: A threat actor group that claimed responsibility for the breach of Grupo Fonsán. Their tactics of publicly naming a victim are consistent with data extortion and ransomware gangs, suggesting a motive of extortion or industrial espionage.
threat_actors.goals: Data extortion, industrial espionage, and financial gain through extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: A likely initial access vector, using a spearphishing email targeting a project manager or engineer, containing a malicious attachment or a link to a credential harvesting page.

attack_patterns.name: File and Directory Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Used by the attacker to map the network and identify file servers, SharePoint sites, or other document repositories where project data is stored.

attack_patterns.name: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility
attack_patterns.description: Used to aggregate sensitive files, such as CAD drawings and financial spreadsheets, into a staging directory, potentially using command-line archiving tools to compress the data.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Used to exfiltrate staged archive files from the network, possibly using a web service like a cloud storage provider to blend in with normal traffic.

tools.name: Robocopy
tools.description: Legitimate tool likely used by attackers to copy large volumes of files from multiple servers to a central staging directory before exfiltration.

identities.name: Grupo Fonsán
identities.description: A major Spanish construction and engineering holding group breached by the 'Booba' threat actor group.

indicators.name: Suspicious Robocopy Execution for Data Staging
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Robocopy Execution for Data Staging
id: f9a8b7c6-d5e4-4f3a-2b1c-0a9b8c7d6e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the legitimate 'robocopy' utility, often abused by attackers to copy large volumes of files to a staging directory for exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-27
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\robocopy.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - ' /s'
      - ' /e'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the legitimate 'robocopy' utility with flags for recursive copying, often abused by attackers to copy large volumes of files to a staging directory for exfiltration.

indicators.name: Creation of Large Archive Files
indicators.pattern:
title: Creation of Large Archive Files
id: e8d7c6b5-a4f3-4e2d-1c0b-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of large archive files (.rar, .zip, .7z), which can be a sign of data staging before exfiltration, especially when created by service accounts or outside of business hours.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-27
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.rar'
      - '.zip'
      - '.7z'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of archive files (.rar, .zip, .7z), which can be a sign of data staging before exfiltration, especially when created by service accounts or outside of business hours.

relationships.target_name: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility
